title: Bartter Syndrome
buzzwords: Infant with polyuria and failure to thrive
Hypokalemic hypochloremic metabolic alkalosis
Salt-wasting tubulopathy with hypercalciuria
Nephrocalcinosis in a dehydrated toddler
High renin and aldosterone, normal blood pressure
Mimics chronic loop diuretic use
Autosomal recessive NKCC2 defect in thick ascending limb
Treated with indomethacin and potassium supplementation

title: Gitelman Syndrome
buzzwords: Adolescent with fatigue, cramps, and salt craving
Asymptomatic hypokalemia found incidentally in adult
Hypomagnesemia with hypocalciuria
Tetany and chondrocalcinosis from low magnesium
Mild salt-wasting with normal blood pressure
Mimics chronic thiazide diuretic use
Autosomal recessive NCC defect in distal convoluted tubule
Treated with spironolactone or amiloride plus oral magnesium

title: Liddle Syndrome
buzzwords: Young patient with early-onset hypertension
Hypertension with hypokalemia and metabolic alkalosis
Pseudohyperaldosteronism with suppressed renin and aldosterone
Spironolactone fails to control blood pressure
Autosomal dominant tubulopathy presenting as secondary HTN
Gain-of-function ENaC mutation in collecting duct
Constitutively active sodium channel independent of aldosterone
Treated with amiloride or triamterene and low-sodium diet
